prompt: Asset type: reader-facing academic systems diagram. Question: What is Claude Code's canonical runtime path and which services branch from it? Draw a clean left-to-right architecture diagram on a warm white background. Main path, exactly once and in this order: CLI / REPL -> Live Session -> Query Loop -> Anthropic Messages -> Tool Router -> Permission Gate -> Sandbox / Shell -> Workspace. Above Query Loop place Context Builder feeding into it, with three small inputs labeled System Prompt, CLAUDE.md, Runtime Attachments. Below Live Session place Transcript JSONL with a two-way persistence arrow. Below Query Loop place Subagents with a secondary dashed connection. Below Tool Router place Events + Traces. Use flat semantic colors: teal interfaces, dark charcoal core, blue model, green capabilities, amber governance, coral execution, gray state. Thick unambiguous arrows, generous whitespace, short readable labels, small simple icons, restrained academic editorial style. Exact visible text only from the listed labels. No gradients, no 3D, no decorative prose, no watermark, no logos, no invented components, no claim that sandbox is always enabled.

Asset type: reader-facing academic flow diagram. Question: How does one Claude Code query iterate? Draw one dominant left-to-right path on warm white: User Input -> Build Context -> Model Stream -> Tool Call? -> Validate -> Permission -> Execute Tool -> Append Tool Result -> Continue Loop. From Tool Call? draw a clear No branch to Stop. From Permission draw Deny to Model-facing Error, which returns to Continue Loop. Add a small upper recovery branch from Model Stream labeled Retry / Fallback and a small lower branch before Build Context labeled Compact if needed. Use rounded rectangles with restrained 4-8px corners, thick arrows, decision diamond for Tool Call?, no crossing arrows, flat teal/blue/green/amber/coral/gray palette, readable labels. Exact visible text only from listed labels. No extra explanation text, no gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no implication that any path was runtime-observed.

Asset type: reader-facing context lifecycle diagram. Question: What reaches the model, and when? Draw four input lanes entering Context Builder: System Prompt with badge STARTUP, CLAUDE.md + Rules with badge STARTUP / LAZY, History JSONL with badge DURABLE, Runtime Attachments with badge PER TURN. Context Builder flows to Token Check, then a decision Compact?, then Model Request. The No branch goes directly to Model Request. The Yes branch goes to Summary Boundary and then Model Request. Model Request flows to Tool Result, which loops back into History JSONL. Keep one obvious left-to-right reading direction and place the return loop along the bottom. Flat academic illustration, warm white background, semantic colors, thick arrows, generous spacing, no crossings. Exact visible text only from listed labels. No gradients, no prose paragraphs, no watermark, no invented memory store, no claim that every feature gate is enabled.

Asset type: reader-facing capability pipeline. Question: How do tools and extensions become model actions? On the left show five compact sources: Built-in Tools, MCP Servers, Plugins, Skills, Hooks. Built-in Tools and MCP Servers feed Tool Pool. Plugins feed MCP Servers, Skills, and Hooks with thin secondary arrows. Tool Pool flows to Visible Schemas, then Model Tool Call, then Tool Router, then Permission Gate, then Execution, then Tool Result, then back to Model Context along a lower return path. Skills feed Model Context, not Execution. Hooks touch Tool Router with a secondary arrow labeled before / after. Use a clean academic systems style, warm white, thick main arrows, short exact labels, simple icons, no crossings, flat teal/blue/green/amber/coral/gray palette. No gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no invented marketplace, no implication that registration always means model visibility.

Asset type: reader-facing trust-boundary diagram. Question: What happens before a tool creates a side effect? Main path left-to-right: Model Tool Call -> Schema Validation -> Permission Rules -> decision Allow / Ask / Deny. Allow goes to Sandbox Enabled? then either Sandbox Wrapper or Direct Execution, both converge on Side Effect. Ask goes upward to User Approval, then returns to Allow or Deny. Deny goes downward to Model-facing Error. On the far bottom show Hooks / Other Subsystems -> Separate Audit Path with a dotted amber line and a question badge, not connected as proven to the main gate. Put a light boundary around Sandbox Wrapper and Direct Execution labeled PROCESS BOUNDARY. Warm white academic style, high contrast, thick arrows, no crossings, exact visible labels only. No shield icon suggesting absolute security, no gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no claim that sandbox is mandatory.

Asset type: reader-facing multi-agent topology. Question: Which child mechanisms exist and what do they share? Place Parent Query at top center, flowing to AgentTool. Split into two large columns. Left column Agent Child contains Own Context, Own Tool Pool, Sidechain JSONL; beneath it show Default Workspace Shared, and beside that an optional dashed branch Worktree Isolation. Label two small modes SYNC and ASYNC near Agent Child, with a note-sized label ASYNC has separate abort. Right column Swarm Teammate contains In-process or Terminal Pane and a durable Team Mailbox below. Team Mailbox has two-way arrows between Parent Query and Swarm Teammate. Result / Notification arrows return from both columns to Parent Query. Use distinct but restrained colors, warm white background, strong hierarchy, thick arrows, no crossings. Exact visible text only from listed labels. No invented V2 mailbox term, no claim every child is a process, no gradients, no 3D, no watermark.

Asset type: reader-facing persistence lifecycle. Question: What becomes durable, and what does resume or fork mean? Main flow: Live Session -> Append Messages -> Transcript JSONL. From Transcript JSONL split to Resume Same Session ID and Fork New Session ID + History. Both flow to Rebuild Live Context. Add Compaction Boundary entering Transcript JSONL from above. Along the bottom place Workspace Files in a separate lane with label NOT A SESSION ROLLBACK; connect Live Session and both restore paths to it using thin arrows, showing that workspace state is external. Add optional Worktree Path as a small durable metadata branch. Warm white academic style, clear left-to-right arrows, flat colors, no crossings, short exact labels. No database icon for JSONL, no gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no claim that resume restores filesystem contents.

Asset type: reader-facing observability and recovery diagram. Question: How are a turn and shutdown observed and recovered? Top lane: Interaction Span contains LLM Span and Tool Span; arrows feed Events + OTel, Query Profiler, and optional Perfetto Trace. Middle lane: Model Error -> Retry Policy -> Retry, Model Fallback, or Surface Error. Bottom lane: SIGINT / SIGTERM -> Graceful Shutdown -> Critical Cleanup -> SessionEnd Hooks -> Analytics Flush -> Exit. Draw a small Failsafe Timer over the bottom lane spanning Graceful Shutdown to Exit. Use three visually separated horizontal bands, warm white background, flat semantic colors, clear arrows, exact visible labels only, readable at document width. No gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no claim tracing is enabled by default, no invented evaluation service.
